mainEntity.name: What if my back just started hurting over time from my job instead of one big accident?

acceptedAnswer.text: Repetitive stress or 'cumulative trauma' back injuries are covered by workers' comp. You must provide medical evidence proving that your long-term job duties caused the back condition.
acceptedAnswer.description:
Yes. In many cases, back injuries aren't caused by one 'pop' but by years of repetitive lifting, bending, or driving. These are known as 'occupational diseases' or 'cumulative trauma' claims. In Utah and Idaho, these are covered, but they are harder to prove because there isn't a single accident date. You will need a medical opinion stating that your job duties were the primary cause of your spinal degeneration.

Key factors include the length of your employment, the physical requirements of your job, and your medical history.

Document your daily work routine in detail. Note how many times per hour you lift heavy objects or how many hours you spend in a vibrating truck seat.

These 'slow-motion' injuries are almost always challenged by insurance companies, making legal representation essential.
